perturbation.original_text: 5. Upon written demand by the disclosing party or upon termination of this Confidentiality Agreement, the receiving party shall: (i) cease using the Confidential Information, (ii) return the Confidential Information and all copies, notes or extracts thereof to the disclosing party wit hin ten (10) business days of receipt of demand; and (iii) upon request of the disclosing party, certify in writing that the receiving party has complied with the obligations set forth in this paragraph. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, 1) neither party will be required to delete electronic Confidential Information stored in back -up/archival storage in accordance with its policies, provided that any such retained Confidential Information will continue to be subject to the terms of this Confi dentiality Agreement until it is destroyed by the receiving party; and 2) a single copy of all Confidential Information may be retained by the Receiving Party’s legal department for dispute resolution purposes only, provided, however, that such Confidentia l Information shall remain subject to the terms and conditions of this Confidentiality Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Upon written demand by the disclosing party or upon termination of this Confidentiality Agreement, the receiving party shall: (i) cease using the Confidential Information. Notwithstanding anything to the contrary herein, 1) neither party will be required to delete electronic Confidential Information stored in back -up/archival storage in accordance with its policies, provided that any such retained Confidential Information will continue to be subject to the terms of this Confidentiality Agreement until it is destroyed by the receiving party; and 2) a single copy of all Confidential Information may be retained by the Receiving Party’s legal department for dispute resolution purposes only, provided, however, that such Confidential Information shall remain subject to the terms and conditions of this Confidentiality Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the obligation to return Confidential Information and copies, it creates an in-text contradiction. While the section still states ceasing of using the information, the removal of return/destroy obligation is contrary to the purpose of an NDA. This undermines the control the disclosing party has over their Confidential Information, contradicting Section 2 requiring reasonable care in protection of the information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Each party acknowledges the sensitive and secret nature of the Confidential Information it will have access to d uring the term of the Project and agrees that it will treat such Confidential Information as strictly confidential and shall exercise the same degree of care in the protection of the Confidential Information as the receiving party exercises with respect to its own proprietary property and trade secrets, but in no event shall it be less than a reasonable degree of care given the nature of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text obligates the receiving party to cease using, return all copies, and certify compliance. The changed text removes the obligation to return Confidential Information and copies, while still requiring them to cease using the information. This creates a contradiction with Section 2, which requires the receiving party to treat the Confidential Information as strictly confidential and exercise a reasonable degree of care in its protection. By removing the obligation to return or destroy the information, the changed text undermines the disclosing party's ability to protect their Confidential Information, directly conflicting with the standard of care required in Section 2. The contradiction is strong because the removal of a key protection mechanism (returning information) fundamentally undermines the confidentiality obligations.

perturbation.original_text: 8. In the event that a party receives a subpoena or other validly issued administrative or judicial process requesting Confidential Information received from the other, the receiving party shall provide prompt actual notice to, in the case of <Vendor Name Here> , the General Counsel of <Vendor Name Here> , and, in the case of Company, to the General Counsel of Company, of such receipt, providing the party with a reasonable opportunity to intervene in the proceeding before the time that the other party is required to comply with such subpoena or other proce ss. If such protective order or other remedy is not obtained, or the disclosing party waives compliance with the provision of this Confidentiality Agreement, the receiving party shall furnish only that portion of the Confidential Information which the rec eiving party is legally required to disclose and shall exercise its reasonable efforts to obtain reliable assurance that confidential treatment shall be accorded the Confidential Information. Notwithstanding anything otherwise set forth herein, <Vendor Nam e Here> or Company may disclose Confidential Information: a) to the extent revealed to a government agency with regulatory or oversight jurisdiction over Company; or b) in the course of fulfilling any of <Vendor Name Here> regulatory responsibilities, incl uding responsibilities under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 (Act) or other applicable law. In either case, <Vendor Name Here> or Company shall furnish only that portion of the Confidential Information which the receiving party is legally required to disclose and shall exercise its reasonable efforts to obtain reliable assurance that confidential treatment shall be accorded the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: 8. In the event that a party receives a subpoena or other validly issued administrative or judicial process requesting Confidential Information received from the other, the receiving party shall furnish only that portion of the Confidential Information which the rec eiving party is legally required to disclose. Notwithstanding anything otherwise set forth herein, <Vendor Nam e Here> or Company may disclose Confidential Information: a) to the extent revealed to a government agency with regulatory or oversight jurisdiction over Company; or b) in the course of fulfilling any of <Vendor Name Here> regulatory responsibilities, incl uding responsibilities under the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 (Act) or other applicable law. In either case, <Vendor Name Here> or Company shall furnish only that portion of the Confidential Information which the receiving party is legally required to disclose.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the requirement to promptly notify the disclosing party's General Counsel about a subpoena, and also removing the requirement to to exercise reasonable efforts to obtain reliable assurance that confidential treatment shall be accorded the Confidential Information this creates a contradiction with the spirit and intent of the NDA, which aims to protect confidential information. Section 3 and 4 emphasizes the need to protect the sensitive information and not share it with 3rd parties. This perturbation makes the NDA weak and hard to enforce
perturbation.contradicted_text: 3. Neither party shall disclose the Confidential Information received from the other to any pe rson or entity, except its employees, officers, directors, independent consultants, affiliates, subsidiaries and other agents (collectively “Representatives”) involved in the performance of the Project, or to use the Confidential Information for any purpos e other than fulfilling its obligations under the Project. Each party acknowledges and agrees that breach of this Confidentiality Agreement by the other and/or anyone employed by or otherwise associated with the other may also constitute a violation of ap plicable laws. Access to Confidential Information by the receiving party shall be limited to those Representatives who have agreed to be bound in writing under terms no less stringent than this Confidentiality Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text removes the requirement for the receiving party to provide prompt notice to the disclosing party's General Counsel about a subpoena and removes the obligation to seek assurance of confidential treatment. This directly contradicts Section 3, which mandates that neither party shall disclose confidential information to any person or entity and limits access to representatives who have agreed to be bound by terms no less stringent than the agreement. The omission weakens the protection of confidential information, making it easier for a party to disclose it without notifying the other party or ensuring confidential treatment, thus creating a direct conflict with the confidentiality obligations outlined in Section 3.

perturbation.original_text: 10. The Effective Date of this Confidentiality Agreement shall be the date it is signed by both parties and it shall terminate upon the earlier of (i) thirty (30) days written notice of either party, or (ii) three (3) years after the effective date.
perturbation.changed_text: 10. The Effective Date of this Confidentiality Agreement shall be the date it is signed by both parties and it shall terminate upon the earlier of (i) thirty (30) days written notice of either party.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the fixed termination date of 'three (3) years after the effective date', and only leaving 'thirty (30) days written notice of either party' to be the termination, this introduces an omission that causes an in-text contradiction with section 11. Section 11 states that 'representations and obligations of each party relative to the Confidential Information shall survive the expiration or termination of this Confidentiality Agreement for a period of the longer of three (3) years or for such time as such Confidential Information remains a protectable trade secret under applicable law'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. The representations and obligations of each party relative to the Confidential Information shall survive the expiration or termination of this Confidentiality Agreement for a period of the longer of three (3) years or for such time as such Confidential Information remains a protectable trade secret under applicable law.
perturbation.justification: The original text specified that the confidentiality agreement would terminate either with 30 days written notice OR after 3 years. The changed text removes the 3-year termination clause, leaving only the 30-day notice. This contradicts section 11, which states that confidentiality obligations survive the agreement's termination for the longer of 3 years or until the information is no longer a trade secret. Since the changed text allows the agreement to terminate in 30 days, it directly conflicts with the 3-year survival clause in section 11. This is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 16. Neither party may assign this Confi dentiality Agreement without the prior written consent of the other party. However, either party may assign or transfer this Confidentiality Agreement and its rights hereunder to an affiliate or successor to its assets or liabilities if such assignee agree s in writing to be bound by the terms and conditions hereof and the non -assignment party is given notice of such an assignment. Any assignment to the contrary shall be void.
perturbation.changed_text: 16. Neither party may assign this Confidentiality Agreement without the prior written consent of the other party. Any assignment to the contrary shall be void.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the clause allowing assignment to an affiliate or successor, this change introduces an in-text contradiction regarding the scope and flexibility of the agreement. It originally allowed assignment under specific conditions (written agreement to be bound, notice to non-assigning party). Removing this, while maintaining the general prohibition on assignment without consent, creates ambiguity. Consider a merger; the original text provided a path for the agreement to continue, which is removed with this change, creating uncertainty about what happens to the confidential information in the event of a merger, which contradicts section 2 and 3.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Each party acknowledges the sensitive and secret nature of the Confidential Information it will have access to d uring the term of the Project and agrees that it will treat such Confidential Information as strictly confidential and shall exercise the same degree of care in the protection of the Confidential Information as the receiving party exercises with respect to its own proprietary property and trade secrets, but in no event shall it be less than a reasonable degree of care given the nature of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The original text allowed assignment to an affiliate or successor under specific conditions. The changed text removes this allowance, creating a direct contradiction with the continued obligation in Section 2 to maintain confidentiality during the 'term of the Project'. The change introduces uncertainty about how confidentiality is maintained if a merger or acquisition occurs, thereby contradicting the reasonable expectation of continued confidentiality established in Section 2.
